executionHistory.evidence: Probed internal/list/... for silent error swallowing and observability gaps. Identified one defect: CompareAggregator.Aggregate in internal/list/reading_aggregator.go silently `continue`d on json.Unmarshal failure, while the parity siblings RecipeAggregator and ReadingAggregator had already been remediated to log+skip in a prior harden round. Rejected as not applicable here: store.go transactions (already use defer tx.Rollback + commit), generator.go (already validates domain consistency), types.go (interface only).

Replaced bare `continue` in CompareAggregator.Aggregate with `slog.Warn("compare aggregator: skipping artifact with malformed domain_data", "artifact_id", src.ArtifactID, "error", err)` followed by `continue`. Verbatim diff in report.md > Code change diff. log/slog already imported by reading_aggregator.go for ReadingAggregator's use, so no import change required.

Added TestCompareAggregator_LogsAndSkipsBadJSON to internal/list/harden_test.go with bytes + log/slog test imports. Test captures slog output to a bytes.Buffer, asserts visibility (warn message + per-artifact-id identification + good-source-not-flagged cross-check) and behavior preservation (1 seed from 2-bad + 1-good input, no bad-source ID leakage). Full test source recorded in report.md > Test code (new).

Adversarial red-then-green proof recorded in report.md > Adversarial proof. With the slog.Warn removed (bare continue reintroduced) the test fails on 3 visibility assertions; with the slog.Warn restored the test passes. Confirms the test is non-tautological and cannot be satisfied without the harden fix in place. Persistent regression coverage lives in internal/list/harden_test.go alongside the parity tests for RecipeAggregator and ReadingAggregator.

No simplification opportunities surfaced. The fix is the minimum viable change: 2 lines of code (slog.Warn call) plus the explanatory comment block. The new test mirrors the structure of TestRecipeAggregator_LogsAndSkipsBadJSON exactly, so no abstraction or shared helper is warranted at this scale (rule of three not yet satisfied). Confirmed by code review of the diff in report.md > Code change diff.

No flaky-test or stability concerns surfaced. The new test is deterministic: no time.Now, no random IDs, no goroutine concurrency, no I/O, no network. slog default-handler swap is wrapped in t.Cleanup so other tests are unaffected by handler state. Verified by repeated invocations: the same red-then-green sequence reproduces deterministically across runs.

No security regression. Logged fields are opaque artifact_id (not PII) and the stdlib encoding/json parser error string (already log-safe; no log-injection vector). The change reduces a hidden-failure class (CWE-391-adjacent silent error swallowing). Recorded in report.md > Audit Evidence > OWASP review and Privacy review.

Full internal/list package suite PASS: `go test -count=1 ./internal/list/...` returns `ok github.com/smackerel/smackerel/internal/list 0.020s`. Verbose parity run of all three malformed-JSON harden tests across recipe/reading/compare aggregators all PASS — see report.md > Validation Evidence. `go vet ./internal/list/...` clean (no output).

Audit checks recorded in report.md > Audit Evidence. No schema migration. No public API change (CompareAggregator.Aggregate signature and return values unchanged). No NATS contract change. No config change. OWASP review: reduces a hidden-failure class (CWE-391-adjacent), no log-injection risk. Privacy review: only opaque artifact_id and stdlib-produced parser error string are logged — no PII surface introduced.

Chaos probe recorded in report.md > Chaos Evidence. Race-detector run (`go test -count=1 -race ./internal/list/...`) PASS in 1.053s — confirms the new slog.Warn path has no race conditions on the package-level default logger. Multi-malformed-input chaos at the unit level: the new test exercises TWO divergent corruption modes (truncated string vs non-JSON garbage) and asserts both are independently surfaced in the captured slog buffer. No new failure modes surfaced; CompareAggregator code path has no shared mutable state and the slog default logger is concurrency-safe by stdlib contract.

No external doc churn required — this is a parity-only internal observability fix. The class of fix is documented in-source via the explanatory comment block above the new slog.Warn call (matching the convention used by the recipe and reading aggregators). No references to CompareAggregator's malformed-JSON behavior exist in docs/ or README, so no doc update is needed. Recorded in report.md > Docs.

scopeProgress.name: Add visibility for malformed product/comparison domain_data
